snapshot.scope: Independent Product Planning review binds the check-only Update Service and its real Updates Settings projection mechanically replayed onto sole main 6acf5b4, including renderer-safe API ownership, machine-local CAS state, stale request/event fencing and release/architecture/settings/trace synchronization without claiming download, install, restart, automatic updates or a production feed.

reviewers.task: Check-only Update Service and Updates Settings contract review
reviewers.independence: Product Planning did not author the Development or UI commits and independently compared their exact contracts, schemas, service, persistence, IPC, renderer projection and tests with API, settings, release, architecture, phase and trace owners on the latest Reader-enabled main.

dimensions.summary: API And IPC Design remains the single renderer boundary owner, while Release Engineering and Settings retain release-flow and machine-local-state ownership; no duplicate update owner document is added.

API, Settings, Architecture, Release Engineering and the accepted auto-update decision agree that the current default is zero-network and check-only; the renderer exposes only typed summary/check/event truth while signed feed, download, install and restart remain future production work.

PIGE-REL-001, B9.13 and E9.09 keep their existing status; acceptance targets record the check-only foundation and truthful Settings projection while preserving signed-feed, packaged alpha-to-alpha and risky-job evidence gaps.

Focused schema, service, persistence, desktop-shell and Settings tests cover body-free DTOs, zero-network defaults, serialization, request/event identity, CAS stale rejection, renderer stale fencing and failure ordering; repository gates remain directly runnable.

The synchronization is confined to existing API, settings, architecture, release, quality, playbook and trace owners plus their semantic lock and independent review recipe; no new owner document is introduced.

verification.command: Independent review of the Development Update Service and UI binding on sole main 6acf5b4
verification.evidence: Review confirms strict body-free summary/check/event contracts, a zero-network default adapter, serialized checks, machine-local CAS terminal state, stale-write ordering and a renderer projection that never fabricates download/install capability; packaged_ready remains only an injected future seam.

verification.command: npm run verify:docs && npm run verify:decisions && npm run verify:traceability
verification.evidence: Links, whitespace, document map, PRD structure, decisions and locked update claims pass with no Requirement, Build, Exit or Phase promotion.

verification.command: npm run verify:contracts && npm run verify:independent-documentation-review && npm run verify:documentation-leanness
verification.evidence: CON-001 through CON-004 remain green; focused update and Settings tests, typecheck and build bind the exact service/contracts/UI while the union recipe retains the already-published Reader, release and security roots.

coordination.taskTitle: Check-only Update Service and Updates Settings binding
coordination.compatibility: The candidate adds a fail-closed check-only Update Service, machine-local status state and typed Settings projection without a production network client, download, install, restart, feed selection, Job or vault data change.
coordination.acknowledgement: Product Planning reviewed the exact Development and UI bytes on the latest Reader-enabled main and synchronized only affected owner/trace contracts; this candidate does not claim a connected production updater, signed feed, automatic update or Public Alpha readiness.

nonBlockingRisks: The default adapter intentionally performs no network request; production signed-feed connectivity and its runtime dependency remain unselected and unimplemented.
No automatic schedule, download, install, restart, risky-job interlock or real packaged alpha-to-alpha update was produced; no Requirement, Build, Exit or Phase status is promoted.
